behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration: source clients + aggregator + summary-input builder. MOCK-MISS: this is not just one function’s pure logic; omission bugs appear when modules are wired together. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch the omission; stubbed source clients are sufficient. LLM-DEP: assertion is on payload passed into summarization, not on model output quality. Rejected higher level (System/Agentic) because behavior text targets inclusion of collected data before/at handoff, not real infra effects or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/report modules in-process, replace the 3 source adapters with controlled fixtures, run collection once, and assert the exact summarization input contains all three source sections/values with correct mapping keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter error -> report composer output). MOCK-MISS: a unit test of one formatter misses orchestration/exception-handling bugs. REAL-INFRA: unavailability can be reproduced by adapter raising timeout/connection errors in-process; real network not required. LLM-DEP: correctness is fallback behavior, not model output quality. Rejected System because behavior text is about graceful degradation logic, which is reproducible with deterministic failing fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process wiring of collector + composer; configure one source adapter to fail (e.g., timeout exception) and two to return data; assert job completes, report contains `data unavailable` for only the failed section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generation quality/content faithfulness, which is model judgment. LLM-DEP: a stubbed summarizer would always pass and hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors. REAL-INFRA alone is not sufficient; the critical risk is real model output variability and fidelity. Rejected Workflow because this behavior describes one agentic decision (single summarization step), not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run summarization against the real model with representative fixtures; assert key numeric/value facts from input are present and not contradicted in output (with tolerance for wording), and repeat across runs/prompts to detect nondeterministic factual dropouts.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is end-to-end real infrastructure: networked source calls/email transport and mailbox delivery semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocked SMTP/email APIs can miss auth/TLS/provider acceptance/inbox-delivery failures. MULTI-STEP agentic chain is not required by behavior text; primary assertion is inbox delivery of the assembled report. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text does not require validating model decision quality, only successful E2E delivery path.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job (or trigger equivalent) against reachable test endpoints and a real test mailbox; verify the email is delivered to configured recipients with expected subject/body structure and includes report sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff across modules/runs: send failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry queue/load. Integration is needed to catch persistence and retry orchestration bugs. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process failing email adapter plus real local test filesystem; real network outage simulation is not strictly required for minimum failure detection. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP agentic do not apply. Rejected System because behavior text is about fallback persistence/retry logic, which deterministic adapter failures can exercise reliably.
behaviors.test_description: Run job with email adapter forced to connection failure; assert report file is written to test filesystem and marked pending. Run scheduler/job again with adapter healthy; assert pending report is retried/sent and local pending artifact is cleared or marked delivered.
